results.classification_rationale: Strong functional evidence of CFTR misfolding (PS3) and consistent clinical presentation across cohorts (PP4).
results.description: Pathogenic variant: deletion of phenylalanine at position 508 of CFTR causes protein misfolding, impaired anion channel function, and cystic fibrosis with high penetrance in homozygotes.
results.notes:
F508del is the most common pathogenic CFTR variant, accounting for the majority of cystic fibrosis alleles in populations of European ancestry.

## Functional evidence

The variant causes [protein misfolding in the endoplasmic reticulum](#cite:Smith2024 "F508del leads to misfolded CFTR retained in the endoplasmic reticulum") and triggers [ER-associated degradation before the protein reaches the cell surface](#cite:Smith2024 "ER-associated degradation removes misfolded F508del before reaching the cell surface").

## Clinical evidence

[Homozygous patients exhibit pancreatic insufficiency in the majority of cases](#cite:Jones2023 "homozygous F508del patients exhibit pancreatic insufficiency in approximately 92% of cases"). [Modulator therapy partially restores CFTR function in these patients](#cite:Jones2023 "elexacaftor-tezacaftor-ivacaftor combination therapy restores partial CFTR function").

papers.rank_rationale: Mechanistic evidence linking the deletion to CFTR misfolding and ERAD.
Clinical cohort evidence for pancreatic phenotype and modulator response.
